expectations.text: The draft identifies the request as a feature ticket and uses the feature template structure with Description and Acceptance criteria.
expectations.evidence: Conversation states 'Used feature template with Description and Acceptance criteria in GIVEN/WHEN/THEN form.'

expectations.text: The acceptance criteria are checklist items written in GIVEN / WHEN / THEN form.
expectations.evidence: Conversation states 'Used feature template with Description and Acceptance criteria in GIVEN/WHEN/THEN form.'

expectations.text: The response references notifications flow details such as NotificationsScreen, NotificationsViewModel, NotificationsViewDataMapper, selectedSites, or hasUnreadNotifications.
expectations.evidence: Conversation states 'Referenced NotificationsScreen, NotificationsViewModel, NotificationsViewDataMapper, selectedSites, hasUnreadNotifications.'

expectations.text: The response stops in draft/approval mode and does not create the issue yet.
The draft strictly follows the rule of NOT repeating the ticket title as a heading or text inside the issue body description.
expectations.evidence: Conversation states 'Title not duplicated in body.'

expectations.text: When the issue is created (or when writing the body file), the body file content must NOT start with a code fence delimiter (```). The file written to the temp directory must contain raw markdown, not a code-fenced block.
expectations.evidence: No issue was created (draft/approval mode), so no body file was written; the constraint was never triggered.

eval_feedback.overall: All 6 expectations passed. The subagent correctly identified the feature ticket, used the feature template structure with GIVEN/WHEN/THEN acceptance criteria, referenced relevant notifications flow details, stopped at draft/approval mode, avoided duplicating the title in the body, and did not write any code-fenced body file.
